subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral (IMF Art IV 2025, World Bank, EC, RSF, TI, V-Dem, Freedom House) + regional independent press (Balkan Insight, OSW) + INSTAT.
A NATO member and EU-accession-negotiating Balkan state riding a tourism boom, with entrenched corruption and steep depopulation the structural drags.

Standout features: tourism-led growth, falling debt, low inflation, and a NATO/EU anchor, against corruption/organized-crime infiltration, captured-leaning media, seismic risk, and depopulation. Source-lean: MODERATE-HIGH EU-accession framing.

economic.notes: Tourism-led growth, falling debt, and low inflation, with tourism concentration the caveat.

fiscal_state.notes: Debt ~54% GDP and falling, with disciplined deficits and tourism-boosted growth. (IMF 2025 Art IV)

monetary_independence.notes: A credible-ish central bank that pulled inflation below target, in a small managed-float economy. (IMF)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The lek is not a reserve currency. (definitional)

inflation.notes: Below target and eased - a clear strength. (IMF 2025 Art IV)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: A tourism boom plus agriculture, hydropower, and remittances - no resource curse, but tourism over-concentration and overheating risk. (IMF; bne IntelliNews)

institutional.notes: EU-driven justice reform from a corruption- and crime-infiltrated base.

rule_of_law.notes: EU accession clusters opened and justice-vetting complete (with elite convictions), against entrenched corruption and organized-crime infiltration. (EC 2025; TI)

statistical_integrity.notes: INSTAT is usable and EU-engaged. (INSTAT; Eurostat)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Improving via EU reforms but clientelist. (EC; OSCE)

press_freedom.notes: Media concentrated in a few politically-connected firms, with opaque state-ad funding and intimidation. (RSF 2025; Balkan Insight)

political_social.notes: Stable one-party-dominant governance with EU momentum, against depopulation and a drug-economy undercurrent.

political_stability.notes: A governing party on a fourth term with EU-2030 momentum, offset by one-party dominance and low turnout. (OSW; Al Jazeera)

civil_liberties.notes: Rated 'partly free,' with formal freedoms pressured. (Freedom House)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: An EU-candidate with lawful routes, hosting an Italy-migration-deal processing centre. (HRW)

social_cohesion.notes: Relatively cohesive, eroded by a drug/organized-crime economy and mass emigration. (TI; OSW)

demographics.notes: A steep decline (down ~1.2%/yr), with severe youth and medic brain-drain. (INSTAT; UNFPA)

geopolitical.notes: A strongly pro-Western NATO/EU-candidate anchor in a stable-ish Balkans.

alliance_reliability.notes: A NATO member and EU candidate, strongly pro-US/Western. (EC; NATO)

conflict_involvement.notes: No active conflict in a stable-ish Balkans. (EC)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: Open and EU-aligning, not sanctioned. (IMF; EC)

physical_practical.notes: Improving infrastructure, against seismic risk and an organized-crime undercurrent.

climate.notes: High seismicity (a deadly 2019 quake), with flood, drought, and heat. (World Bank PDNA)

healthcare.notes: Weak-to-moderate, strained by doctor and nurse emigration. (UNFPA; World Bank)

infrastructure.notes: Improving (EU/World-Bank-financed) from a historically poor base. (World Bank)

crime_safety.notes: Tourist-safe but with a persistent organized-crime/drug undercurrent. (TI; RSF)

disaster_insurance.notes: Low penetration against major seismic exposure. (World Bank DRFI)

summary: A NATO member and EU-accession-negotiating Balkan state riding a tourism boom, with falling debt, low inflation, sound foreign-owned banks, and a strongly pro-Western anchor. The countervailing weight is institutional and structural: entrenched corruption and organized-crime infiltration (EU justice-vetting is underway but incomplete), media concentrated among politically-connected owners, high seismic risk, and one of Europe's steepest depopulation trends from mass emigration. Net, a balanced outcome distribution.
